title: The Mock Interview
description: Simulate a complete job interview. Choose the best answer for each question.

questions.question: Interviewer: "Tell me about yourself."
options: A professional summary focusing on relevant experience and skills
Your full life story from childhood
"I'm a nice person who likes dogs"
questions.explanation: Keep it professional: 60-second summary of your experience, skills, and what you bring to the role.

questions.question: Interviewer: "What are your strengths?"
options: "I'm reliable, detail-oriented, and proactive. For example, in my previous role I..."
questions.explanation: Name 2-3 specific strengths with brief examples from your experience.

questions.question: Interviewer: "What is your biggest weakness?"
options: "I tend to be overly focused on details, but I've been working on prioritizing tasks better"
"My boss was always wrong, not me"
questions.explanation: Name a real but manageable weakness + show how you're improving. Never say you have no weaknesses.

questions.question: "In my ___ role, I managed a team of 10 people and increased productivity by 15%."
questions.explanation: "In my previous role" is the standard way to reference your last job in an interview.

questions.question: Interviewer: "Where do you see yourself in 5 years?"
options: "I see myself growing within this company, taking on more leadership responsibilities"
questions.explanation: Show ambition aligned with the company. Demonstrate you see a future there.

questions.question: Interviewer: "Tell me about a challenge you faced." Which response technique should you use?
options: The STAR method (Situation, Task, Action, Result)
questions.explanation: STAR gives a structured, complete answer that demonstrates your problem-solving ability.

questions.question: "I ___ a new training program that reduced onboarding time by 40%."
questions.explanation: "Developed" is a strong action verb for describing achievements in interviews.

questions.question: Interviewer: "Why should we hire you?"
options: "Because I bring 5 years of relevant experience and a proven track record of results"
questions.explanation: Summarize your value: experience + specific results. Always back claims with evidence.

questions.question: Interviewer: "Do you have any questions for us?"
options: "What does a typical day look like in this role, and what are the team's current priorities?"
questions.explanation: Ask thoughtful questions about the role and team. It shows genuine interest and preparation.

questions.question: Which greeting is most appropriate when meeting your interviewer?
options: "I'm pleased to meet you. Thank you for the opportunity."
questions.explanation: Professional, warm, and grateful. First impressions matter enormously.

title: Estructura STAR
description: Construct STAR answers by ordering the parts correctly.

sentences.correct: I'm a software developer with 5 years of experience in web applications.

sentences.prompt: Build the SITUATION part of a STAR response
sentences.correct: In my previous role at a marketing agency, we faced a tight deadline.

sentences.prompt: Build the ACTION part of a STAR response
sentences.correct: I coordinated with the team and implemented a new workflow system.

sentences.prompt: Build the RESULT part of a STAR response
sentences.correct: As a result, we delivered the project on time and increased client satisfaction.

sentences.prompt: Build a response about your strengths
sentences.correct: I am reliable, detail-oriented, and I take a proactive approach to problem-solving.
Thank you for the opportunity. I'm very excited about this role.

title: Power Words for CV
description: Master professional action verbs for interviews and resumes.

cards.back: Managed / Administered — To describe leadership and management of teams or projects.
cards.example: I managed a team of 15 engineers across three departments.

cards.back: I implemented / I put into practice — To describe concrete actions that you carried out.
cards.example: I implemented a new filing system that reduced processing time by 30%.

cards.back: I Achieved / Achieved — For measurable results and accomplished goals.
cards.example: I achieved a 25% increase in quarterly sales.

cards.back: Developed / Created — For new projects, programs or systems.
cards.example: I developed a training program for new employees.

cards.back: Coordinated — For inter-team work and organization.
cards.example: I coordinated cross-department meetings to align our goals.

cards.back: I led / Directed — Stronger than "managed." For leadership roles.
cards.example: I led the digital transformation initiative for the entire company.
I streamlined the onboarding process, reducing it from 2 weeks to 3 days.

cards.back: I negotiated — For agreements, contracts, conditions.
cards.example: I negotiated a partnership deal worth $2 million.

cards.back: I resolved — For problems and conflicts.
cards.example: I resolved a critical client complaint that saved the account.

cards.back: I spearheaded/led from scratch — For initiatives you started.
cards.example: I spearheaded the launch of our mobile app.

cards.back: Reliable — A quality that shows you can be counted on.
cards.example: I'm a reliable team member who consistently meets deadlines.

cards.back: Proactive — You anticipate problems and act without being asked.
cards.example: I take a proactive approach to identifying potential issues.

title: Question → Answer Type
